summary: Iteration 157 - CRITICAL BUG FIX VERIFIED: Progressive invoicing now correctly calculates percentages on IMPONIBILE (without IVA) instead of TOTAL WITH IVA. All 7 backend tests passed. Frontend UI shows '(imponibile)' labels in the InvoiceGenerationModal. E2E test confirmed: 40% acconto on €9,432 imponibile correctly produces €3,772.80 invoice (not €4,602.82 which was the bug).
test_credentials: Test sessions created and cleaned up after testing
seed_data_creation: Test clients and preventivi created via API for UI testing - all cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 157 fixed a CRITICAL BUG in progressive invoicing. The bug was: percentage calculations (acconto, SAL, saldo) were based on TOTAL WITH IVA instead of IMPONIBILE. This caused DOUBLE IVA. Example: 40% on €9,432 imponibile (€11,507.04 with IVA) now correctly gives €3,772.80 acconto (not €4,602.82). Key changes: (1) Backend /api/preventivi/{id}/invoicing-status now returns total_preventivo as IMPONIBILE. (2) Backend /api/preventivi/{id}/progressive-invoice calculates progressive_amount based on imponibile. (3) Frontend InvoiceGenerationModal shows '(imponibile)' labels in status bar and acconto form.

tests: total_preventivo returns IMPONIBILE (not total with IVA)
total_preventivo_ivato returns full total for display
remaining is calculated based on imponibile
Invoice line has unit_price = acconto amount (imponibile)
VAT is calculated on top of the acconto amount
SAL by selected lines uses line_totals (imponibile per line)
Deductions are based on imponibile amounts

verified: Status bar shows '(imponibile)' label next to total
Created invoice has correct imponibile amount with IVA added on top

bug_verification.original_bug: Acconto % calculated on TOTAL WITH IVA instead of IMPONIBILE, causing double IVA
bug_verification.example_wrong: 40% of €11,507.04 (total with IVA) = €4,602.82 as imponibile, then +22% IVA = €5,615.44
